mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series rise, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns as markets price in near-term earnings upgrades.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real yields and forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation-low-rate regime produces positive forward returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when real rates remain positive.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns, or the signal produces negative returns in >60% of observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annually confirms robust aggregate demand, which directly translates to higher corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above the threshold is followed by negative 60-day equity returns in >50% of cases, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and payroll employment indicate synchronized supply-side and labor market strength, which reduces input bottlenecks and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when the two series diverge, or forward returns remain negative despite synchronized growth in both indicators.
